; CHECK-LABEL: @exactly_one_set_signbit_use1_signed(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[XSIGN:%.*]] = ashr i8 [[X:%.*]], 7
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use(i8 [[XSIGN]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = xor i8 [[X]], [[Y:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP2:%.*]] = icmp slt i8 [[TMP1]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[TMP2]]
  %xsign = ashr i8 %x, 7
  call void @use(i8 %xsign)
  %ypos = icmp sgt i8 %y, -1
  %yposz = sext i1 %ypos to i8
  %r = icmp eq i8 %xsign, %yposz
  ret i1 %r
}

; CHECK-LABEL: @slt_zero_eq_ne_0_fail2(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp ne i32 [[A:%.*]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CONV:%.*]] = zext i1 [[CMP]] to i32
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP1:%.*]] = lshr i32 [[A]], 30
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP2:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[CMP1]], [[CONV]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i1 [[CMP2]]
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %a, 0
  %conv = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  %cmp1 = lshr i32 %a, 30
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %conv, %cmp1
  ret i1 %cmp2
}

tests.test_body:
define <2 x i1> @negative_simplify_splat(<4 x i8> %x) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @negative_simplify_splat(
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <2 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %a = or <4 x i8> %x, <i8 0, i8 -128, i8 0, i8 -128>
  %b = bitcast <4 x i8> %a to <2 x i16>
  %c = icmp sgt <2 x i16> %b, zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i1> %c
}

define i1 @slt_zero_ne_ne_b(i32 %a, i32 %b) {

  %cmp2 = icmp ne i32 %conv, %cmp1

}

  %cmp2 = icmp eq <4 x i32> %conv, %cmp1

issue.title: [InstCombine] `!a == (a < 0)` is not optimized
issue.author: k-arrows

comments.author: XChy
comments.body:
The fold `zext(icmp (A, xxx)) == shr(A, BW - 1)` => `not(trunc(xor(zext(icmp), shl)))` would be a more convenient way to optimize it.
[alive-tv](https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/t7UXuG)
